channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Dynamatic–Aerodata to Pitch ‘AeroForce X’ MALE UAV with Full Indian Design and Software Sovereignty for Tri-Service Tender
item.description: Dynamatic Technologies Limited, in partnership with Germany’s Aerodata AG, is positioning its jointly developed “AeroForce X” as a contender for the Indian Armed Forces’ requirement of 87 Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) UAVs. Valued at roughly ?25,000 crore, the tri-service program is expected to become a defining competition for India’s next generation of ISR and armed UAV capability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Flying Wedge Targets MALE Segment with 1-Ton Kaalbhairav UAV Capable of Armed ISR Missions at 20,000 ft
item.description: India’s emerging unmanned systems ecosystem continues to expand as startup Flying Wedge Defence and Aerospace has revealed plans to develop a 1-ton Medium Altitude Long Endurance (MALE) variant of its Kaalbhairav Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV). The company claims that this larger platform will be capable of operating at a ceiling altitude of up to 20,000 feet Above Mean Sea Level (AMSL), positioning it within a competitive bracket for tactical ISR and light strike roles.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Affordable Precision: Guided Pinaka at 70 Lakh Enhances Indian Army Firepower Edge
item.description: In a major boost to cost-effective artillery modernisation, DRDO has confirmed that the current Guided Pinaka rocket — already in active service with the Indian Army — comes at an exceptionally competitive unit cost of just ?70 lakh. This makes it one of the most economical precision-guided long-range rockets globally. By comparison, the upcoming Pinaka Mk-III Long Range Guided Rocket (LRGR) with 120 km reach is projected to cost around ?1.5 crore per unit, still significantly cheaper than imported alternatives while delivering nearly double the range.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Silent Mobility Push: Indian Army Buys More ‘Silent Scout’ Electric Vehicles for Tactical Edge
item.description: The Indian Army is accelerating the adoption of electric mobility for frontline operations, moving beyond environmental considerations to exploit clear tactical advantages in modern, sensor-heavy battlefields. In March 2026, major tenders (GEM/2026/B/7383148) were closed for a new class of “Silent Scout” electric motorcycles and scooters, signaling a structured shift toward low-signature mobility solutions for reconnaissance and special operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO Developing 300 kW Laser Weapon System with 20 km Range to Strengthen India’s Directed Energy Warfare Capabilities
item.description: India is making steady progress in next-generation warfare technologies as the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) advances the development of a 300 kilowatt Directed Energy Weapon (DEW) system. Designed as a high-power laser weapon with an engagement range of up to 20 kilometers, the system represents a major leap in India’s transition toward precision, speed-of-light combat systems capable of countering modern aerial threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IG Defence Unveils ‘T-SHUL ARC’ Vehicle-Mounted Anti-Drone Jammer for Convoy Protection and Counter-UAS Warfare
item.description: India’s private defence sector continues to expand its footprint in counter-drone technologies, with Defencetech startup IG Defence announcing the development of the IG T-SHUL ARC vehicle-mounted anti-drone jammer system. Designed as a high-power, mobile electronic warfare solution, the system is tailored for convoy protection and the defence of high-value military and strategic assets against evolving unmanned aerial threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Biomimetic Pump-Jet Propulsion: The Silent Revolution India Needs for Next-Gen Submarine Dominance
item.description: Biomimetic pump-jet propulsion is emerging as a disruptive breakthrough in naval engineering, offering a new pathway toward ultra-quiet, highly efficient underwater movement inspired by nature itself. By mimicking the hydrodynamic efficiency of marine creatures such as squid and tuna, this technology replaces conventional propellers with flexible, fin-like blades and ducted flow systems that dramatically reduce noise, turbulence, and cavitation—key factors that determine a vessel’s detectability in modern naval warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: World’s First OptoSAR Imaging Satellite built by GalaxEye launched aboard SpaceX rocket
item.description: While EO sensors capture high-resolution images during sunlight and clear skies, SAR sensors provide all-weather and all-time images, using radar pulses. In a statement, Suyash Singh, founder and CEO of GalaxEye, said, “With the satellite (Mission Drishti) now successfully in orbit, our immediate focus is on completing its commissioning. As we move through this phase, we are already witnessing strong global interest in the differentiated datasets enabled by our OptoSAR payload.”
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: MIDHANI inaugurates its state-of-the-art Aerospace Fasteners Facility
item.description: The facility was inaugurated by Shri Sanjeev Kumar, IAS, Secretary (Defence Production), Ministry of Defence, Government of India, in the esteemed presence of Dr. S.V.S. Narayana Murty, Chairman & Managing Director, MIDHANI. The occasion was also graced by Smt. K. Madhubala, Director (Finance), Shri P. Babu, Director (Production & Marketing), Shri SK Dwivedi, GM (SBD & Marketing), and senior officials of MIDHANI.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: Indian Army contingent departs to Cambodia for 2nd edition of India-Cambodia Bilateral Military Exercise CINBAX-II
item.description: The Indian Army contingent comprises of 120 personnel, majorly from a Battalion of the Maratha Light Infantry Regiment. The Cambodian contingent comprises of 160 personnel, from Royal Cambodian Army.
item.dc:creator: PIB

item.title: Prime Minister congratulates GalaxEye on the successful launch of Mission Drishti
item.description: The Prime Minister noted that Mission Drishti by GalaxEye marks a major achievement in India’s space journey. Shri Modi highlighted that the successful launch of the world’s first OptoSAR satellite and the largest privately-built satellite in India is a testament to the youth’s passion for innovation and nation-building. He also extended his heartiest congratulations and best wishes to the founders and the entire team of GalaxEye.
item.dc:creator: PIB

item.title: Modi govt rattled by Rahul’s Great Nicobar visit; concerns on project must be debated in Parl forum: Cong
item.description: The opposition party claimed that the Modi government is “rattled” and in damage control mode after Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi’s visit to Great Nicobar last week. In a statement, Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said, “The Modi Government, clearly in damage control mode after the hugely impactful visit of the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha, Rahul Gandhi, to Great Nicobar on April 28 2026, issued a press note on the Great Nicobar Island Development Project three days later.” This press note does not address any of the serious concerns that have been raised on it by local affected communities, environmentalists, anthropologists, academics, civil society experts and other professionals, Ramesh said.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Nexus between narcotic dealers, terrorists challenge for country’s internal security: J-K LG
item.description: He also noted that the nexus between narcotic dealers and terrorists had become a challenge for the country’s internal security. “Narco terrorists have targeted our most valuable asset, our youth. They flooded our streets with drugs. We are facing a dangerous enemy, our neighbour (Pakistan) which gave birth to terrorism in the world,” Sinha, who led a foot march against drug abuse here, said.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Representative of Iran’s Supreme Leader in India calls current situation ‘no war, no peace’
item.description: Addressing a press conference here, Ilahi said Iran did not want the conflict, but was compelled to respond amid continued attacks, even as negotiations were expected to continue.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: NIA Court Sentences Let Operative To 7 Years In Bengaluru Prison Radicalisation Case
item.description: The NIA special court in Bengaluru sentenced Vikram Kumar, alias Chota Usman, to seven years of rigorous imprisonment along with a fine of Rs 30,000 under various provisions of the Indian Penal Code, the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, and the Explosive Substances Act.?
item.dc:creator: IANS
